Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Plaitniff'S Opposition To California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF G. S.'s Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Plaitniff'S Opposition To California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Appearances (in court):
David Lilienstein, Esq., of DL Law Group, (415) 678-5050, for Plaintiff.

Joseph E. Laska, Esq. of Hernandez Laska LLP, (310) 742-5817, for Defendant.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff G.S.'s unopposed motion to seal is GRANTED. G.S. moves to seal Exhibits 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 11 to her appendix of evidence in opposition to Defendant Blue Shield's motion for summary judgment. The court finds that the private nature of the documents is apparent on their face. Exhibits 1, 2, 6, and 11 are designated as confidential pursuant to the protective order entered by this court on August 15, 2024. Exhibits 3, 4, and 5 discuss medical information and mental health care treatment given to G.S.'s then-minor daughter, S.S. As to all exhibits that G.S. moves to seal, the court finds that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

A proposed order has been submitted as indicated in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp; CSR: 10619; T: (925) 954-9888; E: raquel@transcriptlegal.com. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication.

The unopposed motion to seal confidential information filed in support of motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is granted and the material conditionally lodged under seal by the moving party shall be permanently sealed. The information requested to be sealed is confidential medical information, protected by federal and state privacy provisions. Moving party has submitted a public version with redactions and has narrowly sought sealing. The court concludes as to the material provisionally lodged under seal, to wit portions of Blue Shield's Exhibits C, D, E, F, G, K, L, and M, that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and is to include the filing date of the document to be sealed, e-filing transaction number, complete title of document to be sealed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
